content:
Key Findings

  • The Finance department at Wyre Council spent £510.4M between 2017/18 and 2025/26
  • Finance accounted for 74.7% of the council's total spending
  • People and Places was the second-highest spending department at £95.7M
  • Resources spent £76.9M, while Transformation spent just £8,000

Finance Dominates Wyre Council Spending

Wyre Council's Finance department spent £510.4M between 2017/18 and 2025/26, accounting for 74.7% of the council's total expenditure. This dominance highlights the central role of financial management in local government operations. The department's spending far outstrips other areas, reflecting its responsibility for core council functions such as payroll, procurement, and financial planning.

The sheer scale of Finance spending underscores its importance. With £510.4M allocated over eight years, the department manages a substantial portion of public funds. This level of expenditure requires rigorous oversight to ensure value for money and compliance with regulations.

Departmental Spending Breakdown

While Finance leads by a significant margin, other departments also play crucial roles. People and Places spent £95.7M, representing 14.0% of total expenditure. This department likely covers areas such as housing, planning, and community services. Resources followed with £76.9M, or 11.3% of the total, which may include facilities management, IT, and other support services.

The Transformation department spent a minimal £8,000, highlighting its limited role in the council's financial landscape. This low expenditure could indicate a focused approach to change management or digital transformation initiatives. The stark contrast between Finance and other departments underscores the centrality of financial management in local government.

Comparing Departmental Expenditure

The following table provides a clear comparison of departmental spending at Wyre Council:

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Finance £510.4M 74.7%
2 People and Places £95.7M 14.0%
3 Resources £76.9M 11.3%
4 Transformation £8,000 0.0%

This breakdown reveals the disproportionate role of the Finance department in Wyre Council's spending. The significant gap between Finance and other departments suggests a need for balanced investment across all areas to ensure comprehensive service delivery.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Wyre Council about the specific initiatives funded by the Finance department's £510.4M expenditure
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about financial planning and oversight
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see how funds are allocated to suppliers
  • Write to your local councillor about the balance of spending between Finance and other departments
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends over time
This article is based on Wyre Council's published spending data covering 2017/18 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
